page.title: Mangusta 80 vs Sunseeker Predator 74: Which Superyacht Wins in Marbella?

data.meta_description: Mangusta 80 vs Sunseeker Predator 74 Marbella: compare size, capacity, amenities & price. Find out which superyacht wins for your charter day. Book now.
data.summary: A detailed comparison of the Mangusta 80 and Sunseeker Predator 74 for charter in Marbella, covering size, capacity, amenities, and pricing to help you choose the best superyacht for your day on the Costa del Sol.
key_facts: Sunseeker Predator 74: pricing on request (not in our fleet)
Both offer licensed skipper, fuel, drinks & snacks included
Mangusta 80 available in grey and white variants
Sunseeker Predator 74 is a sportier, faster alternative

faq.q: Which is bigger: Mangusta 80 or Sunseeker Predator 74?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 is 24m long, while the Sunseeker Predator 74 is 22.5m. The Mangusta has a slightly larger beam and more deck space, making it better for groups wanting to spread out. Both can carry up to 12 passengers.

faq.q: Which boat is faster in Marbella waters?
faq.a: The Sunseeker Predator 74 is typically faster, with a top speed around 38-40 knots thanks to its sportier hull and twin MAN engines. The Mangusta 80 cruises at 28-30 knots, but its top speed is around 32 knots. For pure adrenaline, the Predator wins.

faq.q: Can I charter a Sunseeker Predator 74 in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, you can charter a Sunseeker Predator 74 in Marbella, but it is not in our fleet. We offer the Mangusta 80 (grey and white) and other superyachts. For a Predator, you would need to contact other operators.

faq.q: What is included in the Mangusta 80 charter?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). A jet ski is also included free for the minimum 4-hour booking.

faq.q: How much does the Mangusta 80 cost to charter?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours. This includes the jet ski. For longer durations, pricing is on request. All prices include VAT, skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.

faq.q: Which boat is better for a party in Puerto Banús?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 has a larger flybridge and aft deck, making it ideal for socialising and parties. The Sunseeker Predator 74 is more focused on speed and sporty lines. For a party, the Mangusta's spacious layout wins.

faq.q: Can I visit Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés with either boat?
faq.a: Yes, both boats can anchor at Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés. The Mangusta's shallow draft (approx 1.2m) allows it to get closer to the beach. The Predator's deeper draft may require a bit more distance, but both are fine for those spots.

Both are high-end superyachts, but the Mangusta 80 offers a more traditional Italian luxury feel with teak decks and spacious saloons. The Sunseeker Predator 74 has a more modern, sporty interior. It's a matter of taste.

data.body_html:
When planning a luxury day on the Costa del Sol, few decisions are as important as choosing the right boat. Two of the most sought-after superyachts in Marbella are the Mangusta 80 and the Sunseeker Predator 74. Both turn heads in Puerto Banús, but they offer very different experiences. In this Mangusta 80 vs Sunseeker Predator 74 Marbella comparison, we break down size, speed, capacity, amenities, and price to help you decide which superyacht wins for your charter day.

Size and Capacity

The Mangusta 80 measures 24 metres (79 ft) and can carry up to 12 passengers. The Sunseeker Predator 74 is slightly shorter at 22.5 metres (74 ft) and typically also carries 12 passengers. The Mangusta has a wider beam (5.5m vs 5.2m), giving it more deck space for lounging and dining. If you're a group of 10-12, the Mangusta offers more room to spread out, especially on the flybridge and aft deck.

Speed and Performance

The Sunseeker Predator 74 is built for speed. With twin MAN V12 engines (1,550 hp each), it can reach a top speed of 38-40 knots. The Mangusta 80, powered by twin MTU 16V 2000 M93 engines, tops out at around 32 knots and cruises comfortably at 28 knots. For pure adrenaline, the Predator wins. But the Mangusta's cruising speed is more than enough for a relaxed day hopping between Marbella's best anchorages like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés.

Design and Layout

The Mangusta 80 is a classic Italian open sport yacht with sleek lines and a spacious flybridge. Its layout includes a large aft deck with a dining table, a forward sunpad, and a well-appointed saloon. The Sunseeker Predator 74 has a more aggressive, sporty profile with a lower profile and a cockpit that feels like a sports car. The Predator's interior is more modern and minimalist, while the Mangusta feels more traditional and luxurious.

Onboard Amenities

Both boats come with a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and a full drinks package (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava) plus light snacks. The Mangusta 80 goes a step further by including a jet ski free of charge for the minimum 4-hour booking. The Predator does not typically include a jet ski. For entertainment, the Mangusta has a high-end sound system, while the Predator focuses more on the driving experience.

Charter Pricing

Our Mangusta 80 (available in grey and white) starts at €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours. This includes the jet ski, skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT. The Sunseeker Predator 74 is not in our fleet, so pricing is on request from other operators. Expect to pay a similar or higher rate, often without inclusions like a jet ski. For the best value, the Mangusta 80 is hard to beat.

Which Boat Is Best for Your Group?

Choose the Mangusta 80 if you want a spacious, luxurious day with a jet ski included, ideal for families or groups of friends who want to relax and have fun. Choose the Sunseeker Predator 74 if you're a speed enthusiast who prioritises performance and a sporty aesthetic. For most charterers in Marbella, the Mangusta's combination of space, amenities, and price makes it the winner.

Where Will You Cruise?

Both boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's iconic superyacht marina. Popular routes include heading east to Cabopino and Marbella Marina, or west to Estepona and Sotogrande. Anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for swimming in crystal-clear waters. The Mangusta's shallow draft (1.2m) allows it to get closer to shore than the Predator (1.5m). For more ideas, browse our Marbella boat rental guide.

Final Verdict

In the Mangusta 80 vs Sunseeker Predator 74 Marbella showdown, the winner depends on your priorities. The Mangusta offers more space, a jet ski, and a better all-inclusive price. The Predator offers more speed and a sportier look. For a day of luxury, fun, and value on the Costa del Sol, the Mangusta 80 takes the crown. Check availability and book your Mangusta 80 charter today.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which is bigger: Mangusta 80 or Sunseeker Predator 74?

The Mangusta 80 is 24m long, while the Sunseeker Predator 74 is 22.5m. The Mangusta has a slightly larger beam and more deck space, making it better for groups wanting to spread out. Both can carry up to 12 passengers.

Which boat is faster in Marbella waters?

The Sunseeker Predator 74 is typically faster, with a top speed around 38-40 knots thanks to its sportier hull and twin MAN engines. The Mangusta 80 cruises at 28-30 knots, but its top speed is around 32 knots. For pure adrenaline, the Predator wins.

Can I charter a Sunseeker Predator 74 in Marbella?

Yes, you can charter a Sunseeker Predator 74 in Marbella, but it is not in our fleet. We offer the Mangusta 80 (grey and white) and other superyachts. For a Predator, you would need to contact other operators.

What is included in the Mangusta 80 charter?

The Mangusta 80 charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). A jet ski is also included free for the minimum 4-hour booking.

How much does the Mangusta 80 cost to charter?

The Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours. This includes the jet ski. For longer durations, pricing is on request. All prices include VAT, skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.

Which boat is better for a party in Puerto Banús?

The Mangusta 80 has a larger flybridge and aft deck, making it ideal for socialising and parties. The Sunseeker Predator 74 is more focused on speed and sporty lines. For a party, the Mangusta's spacious layout wins.

Can I visit Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés with either boat?

Yes, both boats can anchor at Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés. The Mangusta's shallow draft (approx 1.2m) allows it to get closer to the beach. The Predator's deeper draft may require a bit more distance, but both are fine for those spots.

Which boat is more luxurious?

Both are high-end superyachts, but the Mangusta 80 offers a more traditional Italian luxury feel with teak decks and spacious saloons. The Sunseeker Predator 74 has a more modern, sporty interior. It's a matter of taste.
